---
title: "Final Project Part 2"
author: "Ziming Xie"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        % Enable wrapping for Pandoc code blocks in PDF
        \usepackage{fvextra}
        \usepackage{float}
        \usepackage{makecell}
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \DefineVerbatimEnvironment{Highlighting}{Verbatim}{breaklines,commandchars=\\\{\}}
editor: source
execute-dir: project
execute: 
    echo: true
---


```{python}
# setup
import geopandas as gpd
from os.path import join
import altair as alt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import time
import re
import warnings
import os

warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
alt.renderers.enable("png")
alt.data_transformers.disable_max_rows()

data_path = 'data/external'

# improve graph resolution
import tempfile 
from IPython.display import SVG, display, Image
import vl_convert as vlc

def display_altair_png(chart, scale=2):
    """
    Render an Altair chart to a PNG and display it inline.

    Parameters
    ----------
    chart : altair.Chart
        Altair chart object to render.
    scale : int, optional
        Resolution scaling factor for the PNG (default = 2). Use scale=2 for standard slides. Use scale = 3–4 for dense figures or PDF exports.
    """
    png_bytes = vlc.vegalite_to_png(chart.to_dict(), scale=scale)

    # Write to a temporary PNG file and display
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=".png") as tmp:
        tmp.write(png_bytes)
        tmp.flush()
        display(Image(filename=tmp.name))
```

```{python}
Path = "/Users/xieziming/Xie-Le-DAP2-Final-Project/data/derived-data"
geojson_path = os.path.join(Path, "District")
final_path = os.path.join(Path, "final_big.csv")
district_gdf = gpd.read_file(geojson_path)
district_gdf = district_gdf[['dt_adcode', 'geometry', 'dt_name']]
final_df = pd.read_csv(final_path)
```

```{python}
final_df['county_code'] = final_df['county_code'].astype('Int64')
final_df = final_df.dropna(subset=['county_lon', 'county_lat'])
def clean_year_data(df):
    df = df.copy()
    df = df[df['year'].isin(range(2004, 2011))]
    firm_both = df.groupby('firm_id')['year'].apply(lambda x: (2005 in x.values) and (2010 in x.values))
    firms_with_both = firm_both[firm_both].index
    
    result_dfs = [df[df['firm_id'].isin(firms_with_both)]]
    firms_without_both = firm_both[~firm_both].index
    df_remaining = df[df['firm_id'].isin(firms_without_both)].copy()
    

    mask_need_2005 = ~df_remaining.groupby('firm_id')['year'].transform(lambda x: 2005 in x.values)
    
    df_2006 = df_remaining[mask_need_2005 & (df_remaining['year'] == 2006)].copy()
    df_2006['year'] = 2005
    
    df_2004 = df_remaining[mask_need_2005 & (df_remaining['year'] == 2004)].copy()
    df_2004['year'] = 2005

    mask_need_2010 = ~df_remaining.groupby('firm_id')['year'].transform(lambda x: 2010 in x.values)
    
    df_2009 = df_remaining[mask_need_2010 & (df_remaining['year'] == 2009)].copy()
    df_2009['year'] = 2010
    
    df_2008 = df_remaining[mask_need_2010 & (df_remaining['year'] == 2008)].copy()
    df_2008['year'] = 2010
    
    df_remaining = pd.concat([df_remaining, df_2006, df_2004, df_2009, df_2008], ignore_index=True)
    
    result_dfs.append(df_remaining[df_remaining['year'].isin([2005, 2010])])
    
    final_df = pd.concat(result_dfs, ignore_index=True)
    return final_df

final_df = clean_year_data(final_df)
final_df = final_df[final_df['year'].isin([2005, 2010])]
```

```{python}
columns_to_drop = ['other_pollutant', 'other_pollutant_name', 'match_method', '_merge', '_merge_final', 'so2_percap', 'so2_intensity']
final_df = final_df.drop(columns=columns_to_drop, errors='ignore')

final_df['city_code'] = final_df['city_code'].astype('Int64')
```

```{python}
firm_2005 = final_df[final_df['year'] == 2005].drop_duplicates('firm_id').set_index('firm_id')[['county_code']].rename(columns={'county_code': 'county_2005'})
firm_2010 = final_df[final_df['year'] == 2010].drop_duplicates('firm_id').set_index('firm_id')[['county_code']].rename(columns={'county_code': 'county_2010'})

migrate_firms = firm_2005.index.intersection(firm_2010.index)
migrate_mask = (firm_2005.loc[migrate_firms, 'county_2005'].values != firm_2010.loc[migrate_firms, 'county_2010'].values)
migrate_set = set(migrate_firms[migrate_mask])

final_df['Migrate'] = final_df['firm_id'].isin(migrate_set).astype(int)

firms_2005_only = firm_2005.index.difference(firm_2010.index)
final_df['Exit'] = final_df['firm_id'].isin(firms_2005_only).astype(int)

firms_2010_only = firm_2010.index.difference(firm_2005.index)
final_df['Entry'] = final_df['firm_id'].isin(firms_2010_only).astype(int)

print("Migrate == 1:", (final_df['Migrate'] == 1).sum())
print("Exit == 1:", (final_df['Exit'] == 1).sum())
print("Entry == 1:", (final_df['Entry'] == 1).sum())

print("\nFirm_id：")
print(f"Migrated: {len(migrate_set)}")
print(f"Exited: {len(firms_2005_only)}")
print(f"Entered: {len(firms_2010_only)}")

```

```{python}
def categorize_firm_type(type_code):
    if type_code in [110, 120, 130, 141, 142, 143, 149, 151, 160]:
        return 'State-owned'
    elif type_code in [159, 173, 174, 190, 210, 220, 230, 240]:
        return 'Private'
    elif type_code in [310, 320, 330, 340]:
        return 'Foreign'
    else:
        return 'Other'

final_df['Firm_Type'] = final_df['type'].apply(categorize_firm_type)


print(final_df['Firm_Type'].value_counts())
print("\nProportion：")
print(final_df['Firm_Type'].value_counts(normalize=True))
```

```{python}
def map_cic2_to_industry(val):
    try:
        clean_val = str(val).strip(" '\"")
        code = int(float(clean_val))
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return "Other/Unknown"

    if 1 <= code <= 5:
        return "Agriculture, Forestry, Animal Husbandry and Fishery"
    elif 6 <= code <= 11:
        return "Mining and Quarrying"
    elif 13 <= code <= 43:
        return "Manufacturing"
    elif 44 <= code <= 46:
        return "Electricity, Gas, Steam and Water Supply"
    elif 47 <= code <= 50:
        return "Construction"
    elif 51 <= code <= 62:
        return "Wholesale, Retail Trade, Transportation and Accommodation"
    elif 63 <= code <= 75:
        return "Information, Finance, Real Estate and Business Services"
    elif 76 <= code <= 96:
        return "Public and Other Services"
    else:
        return "Other"

if 'cic2' in final_df.columns:
    final_df['industry_category'] = final_df['cic2'].apply(map_cic2_to_industry)
    display(final_df['industry_category'].value_counts())
else:
    print("Warning: Column 'cic2' not found in final_df!")

```

```{python}
if 'cic2' in final_df.columns:
    final_df['cic2'] = final_df['cic2'].astype(str)

output_csv = os.path.join(Path, "final_data_clean.csv")
final_df.to_csv(output_csv, index=False)
```

```{python}
district_gdf['dt_adcode'] = district_gdf['dt_adcode'].astype('Int64')
final_df['county_code'] = final_df['county_code'].astype('Int64')

final_gdf = final_df.merge(
    district_gdf[['dt_adcode', 'geometry', 'dt_name']], 
    left_on='county_code', 
    right_on='dt_adcode', 
    how='left'
)

display(final_gdf[['county_code', 'dt_adcode', 'dt_name', 'geometry']].head(10))
final_gdf = final_gdf.dropna(subset=['geometry'])
final_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(final_gdf, geometry='geometry', crs=district_gdf.crs)
display(final_gdf.head())
```
